fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 29-year-old African American male with schizophrenia for medication review.
fields.Subjective: Patient arrived appearing casual and cooperative. Reports taking olanzapine 10 mg nightly consistently using bedtime routine. States doing okay overall. Experiences very rare whispers once or twice monthly which he dismisses. No other hallucinations or paranoid thoughts. Sleep good at 7-8 hours nightly. Appetite healthy, weight stable at 175 lbs. Working part-time as parking lot attendant, steady job for nine months. Mood 'decent' rated 7/10. Lives in supported housing with weekly case manager visits. Enjoys playing basketball at local gym. Attending vocational program exploring career options. Six months sober from marijuana. Aunt visits biweekly. Building independence skills. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Evaluated by James Wilson, LMSW, Social Worker)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed in athletic wear, good grooming

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC and CMP within normal limits.

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives in supported housing; working part-time; six months sober; in vocational program; building independence.
Assessment_and_Plan: Good medication adherence and symptom control.
fields.Author: Note written by James Wilson, LMSW, Social Worker — Independence Housing Services, 2341 Recovery Plaza, Cleveland, OH 44102, Phone: (555) 338-7729, Fax: (555) 338-7730
